title: Dominion Energy Sets Annual Meeting

title: 7 Stocks Ready for a Slowing Economy
text: Although the new year seemingly got off to an encouragingly robust start, recent data suggests that investors should target viable stocks to buy for a slowing economy. At first, such a notion seems odd, particularly in light of the blowout jobs report for Jan. However, this dynamic also implies that more dollars chase after fewer goods – an inflationary scenario that the Federal Reserve hates.

title: Dominion Energy, Inc. (D) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Dominion Energy, Inc. title: Dominion Energy (D) Q4 Earnings & Revenues Surpass Estimates

title: Dominion Energy's Board of Directors Sets 2023 Dividend Rate; Declares Quarterly Dividend of 66.75 Cents
text: RICHMOND, Va. , Feb. 8, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The board of directors of Dominion Energy (NYSE: D) has approved a 2023 dividend rate of $2.67, subject to quarterly determination and declaration by the board.

title: Dominion Energy Announces Fourth-Quarter and Full-Year 2022 Earnings
text: Fourth-quarter 2022 GAAP net loss of $0.07 per share; operating earnings of $1.06 per share Full year 2022 GAAP net income of $1.09 per share; operating earnings of $4.11 per share RICHMOND, Va. , Feb. 8, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Dominion Energy (NYSE: D) today announced unaudited net loss determined in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (reported earnings) for the three months ended Dec. 31, 2022, of $42 million ($0.07 per share) compared with net income of $1.3 billion ($1.63 per share) for the same period in 2021, with net income of $994 million ($1.09 per share) for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2022, compared with net income of $3.3 billion ($3.98 per share) for the same period in 2021.

title: Why Earnings Season Could Be Great for Dominion (D)

title: 3 Industrial Stocks to Buy for a Rebounding Economy

title: Dominion Energy (D) to Report Q4 Earnings: What to Expect
text: Dominion Energy's (D) fourth-quarter earnings are likely to have gained from regulated investments and favorable weather. Share dilution and higher interest expenses will offset some of the positives.
